name: Jesse Kegley
roleLine: Robotics Engineering student at UC Santa Cruz, engineering intern at an aerospace startup, and experienced welder/fabricator.
summary: My experience building my own welding outfit rekindled my passion for learning about how things work and figuring out how to fix them when they don't - which is what I attribute my choice to study robotics engineering to. At UCSC, I've developed experience in programming embedded systems, designing and implementing control systems, hardware design, sensor technologies, and robotic actuation, amongst a plethora of other things.
availability: Open to internships in embedded systems, controls, mechatronics, and manufacturing engineering.

stats.label: AWS Certifications - TIG (D17.1 - Aerospace, Carbon Steel, Stainless Steel, and Aluminum) and Stick (D1.5 - Carbon Steel Unlimited)
Designed Field Oriented Control-based BLDC control system from scratch (software and hardware)
Fully Autonomous Combat Robot designed around PIC32 with custom-made IR sensors and analog circuitry
